title: Wire SandboxAdapter into KERNEL-003 sandbox runner for model-written code (validation lane)
goal: Replace the KERNEL-003 sandbox runner's direct Docker invocation with the SandboxAdapter trait so that model-written code (code emitted by a coder model and executed for validation) flows through the same selection policy as model-process boxing. Preserves the KERNEL-003 ValidationRunner behavior; changes only the boxing layer. Pair MT to MT-049 (which wires model-process boxing); together they enforce 'one sandbox abstraction shared by model-written code, local-model inference, validation runners, and the swarm-agent harness' per cluster B goal.
expected_diff_shape: New src/backend/handshake_core/src/sandbox/validation_runner_binding.rs (the thin bridge); edits to the KERNEL-003 ValidationRunner module (locate via Grep `pub struct ValidationRunner` in src/backend/handshake_core/src/kernel/**) replacing its direct Docker call site with a sandbox_runner: Arc<dyn SandboxAdapter> dependency injected at construction. Tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/validation_runner_sandbox_binding_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: SandboxAdapter trait + selection policy + DockerAdapter merged (DockerAdapter is the KERNEL-003 preservation path).
implementation_notes: Pre-step: Grep the merged KERNEL-003 code in src/backend/handshake_core/src/kernel/** for the ValidationRunner struct AND its current Docker call site (likely `docker_runner.rs` or similar — record exact path for the validator). Strategy: keep the ValidationRunner struct's public API identical so callers in main don't break — only the internal sandbox dependency changes from `Arc<DockerRunner>` to `Arc<dyn SandboxAdapter>`. validation_runner_binding.rs: `pub struct ValidationProcessSpecBuilder` that translates the existing KERNEL-003 ValidationJob (whatever its shape is in main) into a generic ProcessSpec — preserve the existing image/binds/cmd/env semantics byte-for-byte so behavior parity holds. For the default sandbox-runner choice: use whatever the operator's Work Profile knob (`sandbox.adapter`) says, falling back to the registry's default. CRITICAL: model-written code (validation lane) has stricter NetPolicy than model-process boxing — default to NetPolicy::DenyAll unless the validation job explicitly declares network access in its ValidationJob spec (preserving KERNEL-003 behavior). Edits to ValidationRunner: change its constructor signature to `ValidationRunner::new(sandbox: Arc<dyn SandboxAdapter>, ...)` AND add a `ValidationRunner::from_registry(registry: &SandboxAdapterRegistry, validation_job: &ValidationJob) -> Result<Self, _>` factory that selects the right adapter per the job. Update one call site (the Tauri main.rs or wherever ValidationRunner is constructed) to use the factory. Tests: (a) construct a ValidationRunner with a NoopAdapter (test double from MT-042), submit a fake ValidationJob, assert spawn was called with the correct ProcessSpec shape; (b) assert NetPolicy defaults to DenyAll for validation jobs; (c) golden parity test asserts the new ProcessSpec produced by the builder for a known KERNEL-003 ValidationJob equals the Docker argv KERNEL-003 emitted pre-refactor (capture a fixture from KERNEL-003 sources at audit time and store it under tests/fixtures/kernel_003_validation_argv.json).
risk_if_missed: Without this binding the KERNEL-003 ValidationRunner stays Docker-only and the cluster B goal of 'one sandbox abstraction shared by model-written code, local-model inference, validation runners, and the swarm-agent harness' fractures; refactoring MTs (MT-054/055) cannot proceed.
validator_focus: (a) ValidationRunner public API is preserved (no breaking change to callers in main); (b) golden parity fixture matches; (c) NetPolicy default is DenyAll for validation lane; (d) sandbox-adapter dependency is injected (not constructed inside ValidationRunner) so tests can pass NoopAdapter.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Golden parity fixture prevents silent behavior drift when KERNEL-003 ValidationJob -> ProcessSpec translation regresses.
NetPolicy default for validation lane MUST be DenyAll (model-written code is untrusted by default per KERNEL-003 posture).
Public API preservation: any change to ValidationRunner::new signature MUST add a factory method, not break existing call sites blindly.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: ValidationProcessSpecBuilder + ValidationSandboxRunner + select_validation_adapter are implemented and default NetPolicy::DenyAll is enforced; however there is no actual KERNEL-003 ValidationRunner consumer being refactored because, per MT-047 finding and MT-054 migration record, KERNEL-003 has only a non-executing ContainerAdapterStub - the 'preserve existing public API' control is vacuous.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: ValidationRunner binding owned files absent; no src/backend/handshake_core/src/sandbox/validation_runner_binding.rs, no golden parity fixture; KERNEL-003 ValidationRunner remains Docker-coupled, validating the structural concern noted in prior MT-047 reviewer finding ('behavior parity is structurally unachievable').
